description:
Analyze the collagen triple-helix architecture using the crystal structure of a collagen-like peptide, PDB: 1BKV.

Instructions:
1. Fetch PDB 1BKV directly into PyMOL.
2. Identify the three polypeptide chains (A, B, C).
3. Identify all Glycine (GLY) residues that pack the tight core.
4. Identify all non-standard Hydroxyproline (HYP) residues.
5. Measure the distance between the C-alpha atom of a Glycine on Chain A and the C-alpha atom of an adjacent Glycine on Chain B (across the helix).
6. Create a publication-quality cross-sectional visualization showing the three chains in distinct colors, Glycines as spheres to highlight core packing, and Hydroxyprolines as sticks.
7. Save the figure to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/collagen_core.png'.
8. Write a quantitative report to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/collagen_report.txt' containing: the exact count of modeled Glycine residues, the exact count of modeled Hydroxyproline residues, and the Gly-Gly inter-chain C-alpha distance in Angstroms.
